stages.name: unit_tests_failing_first
stages.evidence: Before implementation, task-specific tests exposed the accepted post-125 audit findings: operator-filtered duplicate candidates could disappear, project.root was not effective for list-mutants/doctest flows, invalid run commands mapped through an internal failure path, report config_hash length and selection_preflight skipped evidence drifted from schema, doctest mutation metadata/survivor contracts were incomplete, explicit doctest/advisory-AI config failures fell back too late, AI path redaction and runner failure-kind validation were too weak, and task/schema prose guardrails missed stale claims.

stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: Full suite passed after implementation (564 tests).

stages.name: task_system_validation_active
stages.evidence: Validator passed with task 126 active after implementation and before completion-state edits.

stages.name: task_system_validation_complete
stages.evidence: Validator passed after moving task 126 to complete with active_task and next_task set to null.

residual_risk: Read-side symlink containment is covered by shared guard tests and representative CLI integration paths rather than every possible adapter call site.
Task-system stale-prose validation targets explicit active-task and schema Future claims; broad semantic drift still requires review plus product tests.
AI validation remains structural and provider-neutral; live remote provider behavior is still outside deterministic correctness authority.
